toc.value: 1) List the LEDs exported by your board
2) Read the current trigger and available modes
4) Set the LED to heartbeat mode
6) Make the LED mode persistent across reboots
Option B: Armbian boot environment script
Option C: a user systemd service
7) Why sysfs-level control is often enough

frontMatter.title: How to Control Armbian Status LEDs Through sysfs (Turn Off / Heartbeat on Radxa Rock 5)
frontMatter.author: Amrut Prabhu
frontMatter.summary: Learn how to control Armbian status LEDs via the Linux sysfs interface. This guide shows how to list available LED triggers, turn off the blue status LED, set it to heartbeat mode on Radxa Rock 5, and make the change persistent across reboots.

suggestedArticles.title: How to Tune CPU FAN Speed With PWM on Armbian (cooling-levels based on Temperature)
Radxa Cubie A5E Hands-On: The Budget NVMe SBC
How to Auto-Mount an NVMe Drive on Linux at Startup

prev.title: GL.iNet Slate 7 Pro Review: The Ultimate Wi-Fi 7 Travel Router
prev.author: Amrut Prabhu
prev.summary: Secure your travel data with the GL.iNet Slate 7 Pro, featuring Wi-Fi 7, touchscreen controls, and blazing VPN speeds.
